title: orobanche elatior subsp. icterica
intro: Orobanche elatior subsp. icterica, also known as the Yellow Broomrape, is a parasitic plant that belongs to the Orobanchaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, liver problems, and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of this plant include reducing fever, relieving menstrual cramps, and treating conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. The herbal preparations of Orobanche elatior subsp. icterica typically involve making a tea from the dried plant, which can be used internally to address various health issues or applied topically to soothe skin irritations.
